schema_version: v0 — component-charter shape; migrates into the intention envelope when MAP.md Q6 lands
what: The ONE path to the inference host, and the compile-once gate every inference request passes through. An inference request is a TICKET (a workflow node); the domain runs that ticket's workflow inside the owner: RECEIVE -> CANONICALIZE -> lookup -> (hit) VERIFY -> ANSWER, or (miss) METER -> RESOLVE -> RECORD -> ANSWER. Primitives (cairn/inference_domain/domain.py): `canonicalize(request)` — reduce a request to its canonical form (first cut: structural, sorted-key JSON); `resolve(request, resolver=...)` — run the whole workflow and return {answer, hit, canonical}; `yield_report()` — the meter read back (tokens SPENT on misses vs AVOIDED by hits). The cache is one db_domain-owned, append-only table `inference_calls` (owner `inference_domain`) that is BOTH the compiled answers (verdict='miss' rows carry the resolved answer + its falsifier/horizon/provenance/cost) and the meter (every call lands a row).
why: Telos 1 — demonstrate inference compilation. An answered question must become structure (Law 1): the second time a canonically-identical request arrives, the host is NOT touched — a stored answer is verified (its horizon still holds, T1.3) and served, and that untouched host call IS compilation happening. The domain being the SOLE pipe (Law 6 + Law 4: one owner, reached only through its gate, no other door — the same move as db_domain's sole path to 5432 and the tester's owned network seal) is exactly what makes metering free and complete (T1.2: there is no uninstrumented path to the host), so the saving is a measured fact, not a hope (Law 3). Append-only, on purpose (Law 7): the cache is a log, never mutated — a stale answer is out-voted by verification, not overwritten, which is why db_domain's INSERT-only primitives suffice.
invoke: import: `from cairn.inference_domain import domain`. Prove: `python3 cairn/inference_domain/proofs/test_inference_domain.py` (exit 0 = green), or under the tester via `TesterDevice().run_proof(...)`. Needs db_domain's one-time provisioning (an OS-named LOGIN CREATEDB role); the cache is a db_domain-owned table.
node_class: device (domain) — a spine device by role (the sole path to the inference host). Today it is workflow primitives + the cache; the BaseDevice introspection FACE and bus-fronting are a thin runtime wrapper, a filed edge below.
entry: FRESH design, standard mechanism. The design — the sole-owner gate a third time, the inference request AS a ticket whose states are its workflow, the compile-once cache that verifies before it serves, append-only so the store never mutates a record of truth — is Cairn's, converged with Akien 2026-07-21 (the conversation that corrected 'enforcer' to 'gated ownership' and 'features' to 'workflow states'). The mechanism is ordinary: sorted-key JSON canonicalization + db_domain's owner-gated store; nothing crossed as carried code. The real host client is a LATER wiring (instance-space credentials, a real endpoint), exactly as db_domain filed its live FORWARD path.
state: PROVED for the compile-once workflow, on a real db_domain store, green under the tester (2026-07-21). Teeth passed: a canonically-identical repeat is served WITHOUT touching the host (the injected resolver is called once, then never — Telos 1); a key-reordered request hits the same entry (canonicalize collapses ordering); a stored answer past its horizon is NOT served but forces a re-resolve (verify before answer, T1.3); a hit serves the stored answer UNCHANGED (Law 7); a non-owner write to the cache is refused by db_domain's gate (Law 6); yield_report separates SPENT (misses) from AVOIDED (hits), so the saving is metered not asserted (Law 3). OPEN EDGES, filed not faked: (a) CANONICALIZE is structural only (sorted-key JSON) — it does NOT collapse SEMANTIC equivalence (paraphrases/equivalent prompts); that is T1.3's hard core, wants a /challenge pass + Akien's read before a shape is committed. (b) THE HOST CLIENT is injected (the `resolver` seam) and not yet wired to a real host — it will live HERE and nowhere else; the sole-path TOOTH (a tester import-scan that reds any OTHER module opening the host) is the tester's later tooth, sole by construction today. (c) VERIFY checks the HORIZON only; the stored FALSIFIER is carried for T1.4 active invalidation (evaluating it, and propagating a falsifier upstream, is a later edge). (d) the BaseDevice FACE + bus-fronting (callers poke the domain over the bus rather than importing it) are the runtime face, deferred until a prober/runtime pulls them, as db_domain deferred its face.
gates: proof gate — proofs/test_inference_domain.py, run by the tester. Durability gate: not done until proven AND committed.
falsifier: RED on any of: (1) a canonically-identical repeat touches the host (the resolver is called a second time) — compile-once breached, the whole thesis; (2) a request past its horizon is served from the cache (blind replay — verify-before-answer breached, T1.3); (3) a hit returns anything but the stored answer byte-for-byte (a record of truth mangled on the way out — Law 7); (4) a non-owner write to the cache table SUCCEEDS (Law 6 breached); (5) a call does not land a meter row, or yield_report miscounts SPENT vs AVOIDED (the meter is incomplete — Law 3, T1.2); (6) any module OTHER than inference_domain opens the inference host (sole-path breached — enforced by the tester import-scan once that tooth exists). Wrong-shape signal: if metering/caching turn out to need to live INSIDE the resolver loop rather than at this domain boundary, 'the domain' was the wrong seam.
how_it_learns: Directly, and it is the first device whose learning is its point. The meter IS the learning surface: `yield_report` turns each call into evidence — tokens AVOIDED climbing against tokens SPENT is the measured answer to 'is inference compilation paying off?' (Telos 1, Law 3). Its own horizon: today it learns WHETHER the cache pays (the yield); it does not yet learn WHICH questions are the same (semantic canonicalization, T1.3) or WHEN an answer has gone wrong (active falsifier invalidation, T1.4) — those are the two named edges where the learning deepens as real inference traffic pulls them. The librarian above it (the next spine steps) is the first heavy consumer that will pull them.
traces_to: Telos 1 (demonstrate inference compilation — T1.1 answers-as-structure, T1.2 every call metered because this is the sole pipe, T1.3 a hit verifies before it answers, T1.4 falsifier+horizon on every answer)
Law 6 (exactly one owner; reached only through the owner's gate — the sole path to the host)
Law 4 (physics over policy — sole path by construction now, by tester import-scan later; gates not supervisors)
Law 7 (append-only cache — a record of truth is never mutated)
Law 3 (the meter makes the saving measured; a stored answer carries its own expiry)
Law 1 (an answered question becomes structure, not a re-derivation)
build order: inference domain, after the heartbeat+bus+system-device block (MAP.md:888)
owner: CC owns inference_domain's code + proofs and gates writes to them. Akien owns the design ratification (the inference-request-as-ticket framing and the sole-owner-gate model, converged 2026-07-21) and the node-class definitions. The cache table `inference_calls` is owned (in db_domain) by `inference_domain`.
proof: proofs/test_inference_domain.py — compile-once (a repeat does not touch the host); canonical equivalence (key order is the same question); verify-before-answer (a stale entry re-resolves); the answer is served unchanged (Law 7); the owner gates the cache (Law 6); the meter measures spent against avoided (Law 3). Run by the tester on a real db_domain store; self-cleaning (an ephemeral table, dropped). Green: `python3 cairn/inference_domain/proofs/test_inference_domain.py`.
provenance: Born 2026-07-21 as the next step of the code spine after the heartbeat+bus+system-device block, in a design conversation with Akien that reframed the device twice before a line was written: an inference request is a TICKET whose workflow-states are the device's insides (not a bag of features), and the 'enforcer' that makes those states physics is not a supervisor but a gated resource only one owner reaches (the db_domain pattern, a third time). Built UNDER the tester and dogfooded on db_domain, exactly as db_domain was. Advances Telos 1 from thesis toward demonstrated; does not yet retire CLAUDE.md's 'the inference host is reached only through inference_domain' IOU — that retires when the tester import-scan makes sole-path physics rather than construction.
